reviews.comment:
Yo era habitué de este restó desde q se llamaba Caballito Blanco Cambiaron la concesion y con ello, a mi entender, bajo mucho la calidad y perdió todo el glamour. Las medialunas son mucho mas chicas, ya no hay pan Madrileño, los sandwichs tienen poco fiambre y las tazas de cafe con leche  -que eran su punto  fuerte - las cambiaron por otras mas chicas en forma de triangulo, que parece un cortado en jarrito. Los precios altos, la calidad mediocre y la cantidad paupérrima. Obviamente , mi proxima parada no sera alli, con seguridad. Se percibe un manejo nada artesanal, sino totalmente financiero. Soy viajero frecuente de la zona. Para descartar.

(Translated by Google)
I was a regular at this restaurant back when it was called Caballito Blanco. They changed the concession, and with that, in my opinion, the quality dropped significantly, and it lost all its charm. The croissants are much smaller, they no longer have Madrid-style bread, the sandwiches have very little cold cuts, and the coffee cups with milk—which used to be their strong point—have been replaced with smaller, triangular ones that look like a cortado in a small mug. The prices are high, the quality mediocre, and the portions meager. Obviously, my next stop won't be there, that's for sure. It feels like it's not run by artisans at all, but rather entirely for profit. I'm a frequent traveler in the area. Definitely not for me.
reviews.name: accounts/109853746269412609224/locations/14840228736131746142/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNjdHEyaWt3RRAB
